title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI's No. 2 role to focus on a chronic illness
subtitle: The applications chief and possible future CEO will transition to a part-time advisor role. Greg Brockman has been running product in her absence since April.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Fidji Simo announced on July 9, 2026 that she is stepping down from her role as OpenAI's CEO of Applications to focus on her health. Simo joined OpenAI from Instacart in May 2025, and until this week was widely regarded as Sam Altman's number two and a possible future chief executive. She has been on medical leave since April; OpenAI President Greg Brockman took over product responsibilities during her absence. Simo will transition to a part-time advisor role and no successor has been named.

In her memo, Simo disclosed that she has Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome — POTS — diagnosed in 2019, and that a recent flare made a senior operating role impossible to sustain. The Applications organization she leaves runs ChatGPT, the consumer subscription tiers, the API business, and the enterprise sales function that together generate the majority of OpenAI's roughly $2 billion in monthly revenue. Its next leader inherits both the growth engine and the operational load of a company preparing for a possible 2026 or 2027 public offering.

The departure continues a run of senior turnover at OpenAI in 2026 that has now touched research, safety, policy, and applications leadership. It lands the same week Apple filed a trade-secret suit alleging OpenAI ran a coordinated campaign to hire more than 400 former Apple engineers, and one week after Fortune reported Anthropic had overtaken OpenAI on annualized revenue. A company under simultaneous legal, competitive, and organizational pressure is a different company than the one that shipped GPT-5 last year, and the picture is more concrete this week than it was two weeks ago.

Takeaway for learners: senior tenure at frontier AI labs is short — much shorter than at the FAANG companies most of these leaders came from. If you are joining one of these organizations, do not build your career around a specific manager or org chart. Build it around the technical or product problem you want to work on, and expect the reporting lines above you to reshuffle on a 12–18 month cadence.

sources.title: OpenAI exec Fidji Simo says she's stepping down due to chronic illness — CNBC
